evals.prompt: Generate a Snappi test script for a simple port-to-port traffic test. OTG config has 2 ports (P1, P2) with 1 flow from P1 to P2. Infrastructure: controller at 10.0.0.1:8443, P1 maps to eth1, P2 maps to eth2. Test duration 30 seconds, metrics every 5 seconds. No assertions.
evals.expected_output: Standalone Python Snappi script with all necessary imports, API connection, config loading, traffic start/stop, metrics collection, and interactive prompts
assertions: Output is valid Python code (no syntax errors)
Script imports snappi module
Script initializes API client with correct controller IP and port
Script loads OTG configuration
Script has start_traffic() and stop_traffic() functions
Script collects metrics with correct interval (5 seconds)
Script includes interactive prompt ('Press Enter to START TRAFFIC')
Script has error handling and retry logic with backoff
Script is standalone and immediately runnable (no external dependencies besides snappi)
Script has proper cleanup (stop traffic, stop protocols) in finally block
Script includes JSON report generation

evals.prompt: Generate a Snappi test script for BGP convergence testing. OTG config: 2 ports with BGP devices (AS 101 and AS 102). Infrastructure: controller 192.168.1.100:8443, P1->eth1, P2->eth2. Test duration 60 seconds, metrics every 5 seconds. Assertions: BGP sessions equals 2, packet loss less than 0.01%.
evals.expected_output: Python Snappi script with BGP protocol startup, assertion validation, and metrics collection for convergence testing
assertions: Output is valid Python code
Script includes protocol startup logic (start_protocols function)
Script waits for protocol convergence before starting traffic
Script includes assertion validation with correct operators
Script validates BGP session count assertion
Script validates packet loss assertion
Script displays assertion results with PASS/FAIL status
Script logs detailed metrics including tx_frames, rx_frames, loss_pct
Script handles missing protocols gracefully
Test duration is 60 seconds
Metrics interval is 5 seconds

evals.prompt: Generate a Snappi test script with custom infrastructure YAML inline. OTG config for 2-port bidirectional flows. Infrastructure: controller 10.20.30.40 port 443 http protocol, ports P1/P2 mapped to 1/1/1 and 1/1/2. Test 45 seconds, metrics every 3 seconds. Assertions: tx frames > 50000, rx frames > 50000.
evals.expected_output: Python script that parses inline infrastructure config and generates appropriate Snappi code
assertions: Script uses correct controller protocol (http not https)
Script uses correct port locations (1/1/1, 1/1/2 format)
Script uses correct test duration (45 seconds)
Script uses correct metrics interval (3 seconds)
Script includes flow frame count assertions
Script validates both tx and rx frames separately
Script generates valid Python with all necessary config embedded

evals.prompt: Generate a Snappi test script with multiple flows and assertions. OTG config has 4 flows (flow1, flow2, flow3, flow4) between 2 ports. Infrastructure: standard setup. Assertions for each flow: validate tx/rx frames, check latency < 10ms. Include error handling and retry logic.
evals.expected_output: Snappi script handling multiple flows with per-flow assertions and robust error handling
assertions: Script handles multiple flows correctly
Script collects metrics for all flows
Script includes latency assertions
Script validates assertions per-flow
Script has exponential backoff retry logic
Script catches and handles exceptions gracefully
Script displays detailed error messages
Script cleans up resources even on failure

evals.prompt: Generate a Snappi test script for a complex test scenario: BGP with ISIS backup, multiple traffic flows, capture configuration, and dynamic rate changes. Infrastructure includes controller at 10.0.0.1, 3 ports. Duration 120 seconds.
evals.expected_output: Comprehensive Snappi script for advanced traffic testing with protocol redundancy and traffic control
assertions: Script handles multiple protocols (BGP and ISIS)
Script includes port capture configuration if specified
Script is extensible for future enhancements
Script includes comprehensive logging
Script has clear section separation (Config, API Functions, Main Execution)
Script includes docstrings for all functions
Script supports 120-second duration
Script structure allows for future additions without major refactoring
